title: Employer/Union Rights and Obligations — NLRB
description: NLRB page cataloguing reciprocal employer and union obligations under the NLRA: prohibited employer conduct (threats, surveillance, benefit promises, retaliatory discipline), prohibited union conduct (coercion, discriminatory discipline, picket-line misconduct), the duty to bargain in good faith over mandatory subjects, contract-modification notice requirements, and union-security/Beck rules in non-right-to-work states.
notes: CONTRACT-MODIFICATION DEADLINES: written notice to the other party 60 days before expiration/proposed change; FMCS notice within 30 days of that; no strike/lockout for 60 days after notice or until expiration (whichever is later). Health-care institutions have different timelines (NLRA §§8(d)(4), 8(g)). Massachusetts is NOT a right-to-work state.
